source: User stated CodeWiki should learn iteratively: new requests should check existing tasks/sprints and refine them instead of creating new tasks every time.

diff_table.current_state: Agents often create a new roadmap task for each user request, and codewiki_task create only reuses an existing task when an explicit id is supplied.
diff_table.desired_state: Agents and backend task creation should check active tasks/sprints for related intent and refine matching work instead of creating duplicates.
diff_table.rationale: CodeWiki is intended to accumulate knowledge progressively about the user and project rather than rebuilding task/doc structure from scratch on every request.
diff_table.risk: Over-eager matching could merge unrelated work; matching must require more than broad scope overlap and exclude closed/cancelled tasks unless explicitly targeted.
alternatives: Keep behavior in agent prompt guidance only.
Require explicit task ids for all refinement.
Add semantic embeddings later for stronger matching.

accepted_decisions.summary: Treat related user requests as additive refinements of active roadmap work by default.
Use deterministic matching first: overlapping spec paths, code paths, labels, research ids, titles, and intent tokens.
Do not auto-refine closed/cancelled tasks without explicit ids.

assumptions: Active task matching can start with deterministic heuristics and improve later.
non_goals: No duplicate detection UI in this task.

knowledge: Document progressive refinement in roadmap/compiler/API/adapter specs.
code: Implement deterministic active task reuse/refinement in codewiki_task create.

knowledge: Document progressive refinement in roadmap/compiler/API/adapter specs.
code: Implement deterministic active task reuse/refinement in codewiki_task create.
